1 Year Investment Plan3 Year Investment Plan5 Year Investment Plan10 Year Investment Plan

What is an Investment Plan?

Investment Plans are essentially financial instruments that help create sustainable wealth for the future. Various investment plans in India enable us to invest our savings into different money-market products in a disciplined and periodic manner to achieve our financial goals.

Overall, investment plans provide the much-needed advantage of maximizing our savings through systematic, long-term investments and create wealth for the future. The first step towards having the best investment plan in India is to assess your risk profile and financial needs, and then choose a best investment plan that aligns with your needs.

Investment Plans are essentially financial instruments that help create sustainable wealth for the f…

Read More

Guaranteed Plans

Guaranteed plans are typically favoured by conservative individuals who focus on generating assured returns from their investments. Some key benefits of guaranteed investment plans are:

  • Capital Protection

    Investments plans that offer assured returns ensure that the principal amount invested is not at any risk. This ensures that the original capital invested is protected at all times.

  • Low Risk

    Returns from guaranteed investment plans are not impacted by changing market conditions. This helps in reducing the overall risk associated with the investment.

  • Tax Benefits

    Many guaranteed plans offer tax benefits to the investor both at the time of making the investment and the at maturity. This can potentially increase net returns of the plan.

  • Suitability

    The low risk and capital protection benefits offered by guaranteed plans are considered suitable by individuals with low risk appetite. Examples include retired individuals who want a regular income at minimal risk.

Guaranteed plans are typically favoured by conservative individuals who focus on generating assured returns from their investments. Some key benefits of guaranteed investment plans are:

  • Capital Prot…

46 Best Investment Plans in India 2026

Currently, prospective investors in India are spoilt for choice if they decide to seek out investment plans that suit their unique financial needs. Below is a list of 46 popular investment options in India along with their tax benefits, wherever applicable:

Currently, prospective investors in India are spoi…

Read More

Low Risk Investment Plans

1. Public Provident Fund (PPF)

This government-backed scheme features minimal risk and provides assured returns to the investor. [Public Provident Fund (PPF)](/blog/investments/what-is-ppf) account matures in 15 years and investments made into the account provide tax benefits u/s 80C up to the cumulative limit of Rs. 1.5 lakh annually. Additionally, the interest earned and maturity amount of PPF is also tax exempt.

  • Tax Deductions Under Section 123

    Under Section 123 of Income Tax Act, 2025 (earlier Section 80C of Income Tax Act, 1961) tax deduction benefits up to ₹1.5 lakh annually can be availed in a tax year, provide one opts for the old tax regime.

  • Long-Term Investment Benefit

    PPF account matures in 15 years are offers assured returns over its entire investment period. This ensures that returns are predictable making it suitable for achieving various long-term goals.

  • No Tax on Withdrawals

    As a EEE investment, PPF offers the benefit of exemption on the maturity amount. This means, no tax is applicable on PPF withdrawals at maturity, which increases the potential benefit from the investment.

2. Capital Guarantee Plans

Capital Guarantee Plans are a type of life insurance plan which combines the benefit of life insurance along with the security of the principal amount invested, while retaining the potential for market-linked returns. Usually these plans are often a combination of 2 different plans – a unit linked insurance plan and a guaranteed income plan with the investment getting distributed between the two plans as per the policy details. This is considered suitable for individuals with low risk appetite since, the principal invested is never at risk.

  • Disciplined Long-Term Investing

    Capital Guarantee Solutions are long-term investments that require regular premium payments from the policyholder. This can help inculcate a habit of making disciplined long-term investments in the policyholder.

  • Secure Financial Planning Solution

    Capital guarantee solutions ensure protection of the principal and also provide market-linked long-term growth. This makes this investment plan suitable as a financial planning tool capable of achieving a range of financial goals.

  • Wealth Preservation

    Capital Guarantee Solutions ensure that the principal invested is never at any risk. This way, the investment plan ensures preservation of wealth for the policyholder over various time periods.

3. Guaranteed Savings Plan

Guaranteed Savings Plans, as a name suggests, is a type of life insurance plan that offers policyholders assured returns along with in-built life cover benefits. The sum assured as per the policy documents are payable to the policyholder at maturity, if the policyholder survives the policy term. In case of demise of the policyholder during the policy term, death benefits are payable to the nominee/beneficiary as per the terms and conditions of the policy.

  • Stable and Predictable Growth

    The assured returns of the Guaranteed Savings Plan are communicated at the time of purchase. This ensures stable and predictable growth of investments for the policyholder.

  • Financial Security for Future Goals

    The low risk typically associated with guaranteed investment plans ensures that the principal invested is at minimal risk for the investor. This allows policyholders to pursue their future financial goals with minimal risk.

  • Disciplined Savings Habit

    Guaranteed investment plans allows policyholders to make regular investments over the long-term. These regular investments help inculcate a habit of making disciplined investments in the policyholder.

4. Post Office Monthly Income Schemes

[Post Office Monthly Income Scheme](/blog/investments/post-office-monthly-income-scheme) is a post office savings scheme that offers fixed returns and can be started with a minimum investment of ₹1000. In case of sole account, the maximum investment allowed is ₹9 lakh, whereas, in the case of joint account, the maximum investment allowed is ₹15 lakh. This account matures and can be closed after completion of 5 years.

  • Stable and Predictable Returns

    POMIS Account offers a fixed rate of returns that is revised periodically, usually on a quarterly basis. This ensures that the investor received stable and predictable returns from the investment plan.

  • Low-Risk Savings Option

    POMIS is backed by the Government of India and features sovereign guarantee. This ensures that the account balance is at minimal risk and is not impacted by changing market conditions.

  • Regular Cash Flow Benefits

    POMIS accounts provide regular monthly payouts to the depositor as per the prevailing interest rate. This ensures the benefit of regular cash at all times for the investor.

    • 5. Fixed Deposit (FD)

    [Fixed Deposit (FD)](/investment-plans/fixed-deposit) have been a popular assured return investment option in India for decades now. While returns from this investment is predictable, only tax saver FD offer tax benefits to the investor. Other FDs including post office savings schemes do not offer tax benefits and their returns are also taxable.

    • Low-Risk Savings Option

      Fixed Deposits of up to ₹5 lakh held with banks in India are insured by the DICGC, similarly post office FD features sovereign guarantee. This makes FD a relatively low-risk savings option in India.

    • Flexible Tenure Options

      FD can be opened with a tenure as short as 7 days and for as long a period as 10 years. This flexibility helps ensure that FD can be used to achieve a wide range of short, medium and long-term investment goals.

    • Reliable Income Generation

      FD rates are fixed at the time of account opening and remain unchanged over the entire tenure of the FD. This allows fixed deposits to generate income in a predictable and reliable manner over the entire tenure of the investment.

    6. Senior Citizens Savings Scheme

    This investment instrument is currently only available to senior citizens and those who are planning to retire soon. [Senior Citizens Savings Scheme](/blog/retirement-planning/senior-citizen-savings-scheme) investment account provides fixed returns making it a predictable low risk investment suitable for providing regular income post-retirement. Additionally, both the initial investment made and the interest earned are tax-free.

    • Regular Income After Retirement

      SCSS investments provide predictable growth of investments. This ensures that eligible senior citizen investors receive regular income post-retirement to secure their finances.

    • Government-Backed Investment Safety

      SCSS is a government backed small savings scheme, so it features sovereign guarantee. This means that the principal is at low risk and the investment is among the safest investment options currently available in India.

    • Easy Investment and Withdrawal Options

      SCSS accounts can be opened in most major banks in India as well as at the India Post Office. This is designed to make SCSS easy to invest in. Withdrawal rules of the plan are also simple, which helps in easy adoption of this investment plan.

    • Reliable Retirement Planning Option

      SCSS offers assured returns that can serve as a source of reliable regular income for retirees who no longer receive regular income from salary or business.

    7. RBI Bonds

    RBI Bonds also known as RBI floating rate savings bonds are a type of debt-oriented scheme issued by the Reserve Bank of India and featuring the sovereign guarantee of the Government of India. Unlike many other types of bonds, these floating rate bonds can undergo a change in coupon rate every 6 months. Featuring a maturity period of 7 years, these are considered as a low-risk investment due to Government of India’s sovereign guarantee. As per current tax rules, there is no tax benefit of making this investment and returns are subject to applicable capital gains tax rules.

    • Stable Long-Term Returns

      RBI Floating Rate Bonds provide returns based on their coupon rate. This means that these bonds provide assured returns even though the rate of returns keeps changing based on market conditions.

    • Capital Protection Benefits

      These RBI bonds offer the benefit of sovereign guarantee as they are backed by the Government of India. This means that the initial investment made by the investor is at minimal risk and their seed capital is protected at all times.

    • Suitable for Conservative Investors

      Due to the twin benefits of low risk and assured returns, the potential for loss is minimised in the case of this investment plan. That’s why RBI bonds are often preferred by conservative investors who prioritise safety of capital over potentially high returns.

    8. National Savings Certificate (NSC)

    [National Savings Certificate (NSC)](/investment-plans/national-savings-certificate) is a government-backed small savings scheme that matures in 5 years. This is a low-risk investment that provides assured returns that are locked-in at the time of investment. The initial NSC investment and interest earned for first 4 years qualify for Section 80C tax benefits, however, maturity proceeds of this instrument are taxable.

    • Stable Wealth Growth

      • NSC interest rates are fixed by the Ministry of Finance and subject to revision on a quarterly basis. This ensures that NSC investments grow at a predicable rate over time.

    • Fixed Interest Earnings

      • NSC offers returns at a fixed interest rate that are fixed at inception and remains unchanged over the investment tenure. So you can estimate the growth of your investments accurately as soon as you invest.

    • Low-Risk Investment Opportunity

      • NSC is a government-backed scheme, so it features sovereign guarantee. This means that this investment option features a relatively low level of risk.

    9. Sovereign Gold Bonds (SGB)

    SGB are Government of India backed bonds that are benchmarked to the price of gold in India. While these bonds offer an excellent way to diversify investment through exposure to gold in a cost-effective manner, no tax benefit is available from these.

    • Fixed Interest Earnings

      SGB offer assured fixed interest payouts during the period of investment. This ensures that individuals buying these bonds receive regular income while they stay invested.

    • Tax Benefits on Redemption

      SGB currently mature after 7 years from the date of issuance. At maturity, the returns are based on the market price of gold at the time of maturity. The returns from SGB at maturity are tax exempt subject to applicable terms and conditions.

    • Hedge Against Inflation

      Since SGB value is dependent on the market price of gold, this investment option provides a similar degree of inflation protection as physical gold holdings.

    • Secure Digital Gold Investment

      SGB is backed by the Government of India, so features the benefit of sovereign guarantee. So, SGBs offer a highly secure way to benefit from digital investments made in gold.

    10. Monthly Income Plans

    Monthly Income Plans are a type of investment plan that are designed to provide a monthly payout to the investor over a pre-determined time period. This type of plan is often preferred by retired individuals who are seeking regular income from their investments. The potentially low risk associated with MIPs make this plan a suitable investment option for investors with low risk appetite. Currently these investment plans are offered by a wide range of financial entities ranging from life insurance companies to the India Post Office.

    • Steady Monthly Cash Flow

      This investment plan provide monthly payouts to the investor. This way, one gets access to a steady monthly cash flow based on the prevailing interest rate and the amount invested.

    • Predictable Income Generation

      The interest rate offered by monthly income plans is fixed at inception and remains unchanged over the duration of the investment. This means that the monthly payouts from MIP are predictable and remain unchanged over the duration of the investment plan.

    • Financial Stability Support

      MIP provides secure monthly income to the investor. This translates to adequate financial stability for the investor. This makes MIP a suitable option for those seeking passive regular income from their investments.

    11. Atal Pension Yojana (APY)

    [Atal Pension Yojana](/blog/retirement-planning/what-is-atal-pension-yojana) is a government-backed scheme that is overseen by the PFRDA (Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority). APY is primarily designed to provide regular guaranteed pension of Rs. 1000 to Rs. 5000 per month to individuals who are working in the unorganised sector. As per current tax rules, APY investments are eligible for tax deduction of up to Rs. 2 lakh annually under the old tax regime similar to NPS.

    • Guaranteed Pension After Retirement

      APY scheme is designed to provide guaranteed pension ranging from ₹1000 to ₹5000 after the subscriber attains the age of 60 years.

    • Financial Stability for Old Age

      The fixed pension payout is designed to be disbursed throughout the lifetime of the subscriber. This can help provide financial stability for old age and reduce the risk of outliving savings.

    • Social Security for Unorganised Sector

      Being targeted primarily at workers in the unorganised sector, the primary benefactors of APY are expected to individuals who are not engaged in any formal employment.

    12. Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana

    [Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana](/blog/investments/sukanya-samriddhi-yojana) available to parents of a girl child, this tax saving investment options offers Section 80C benefits on the amount invested and the maturity proceeds received after the girl child reaches 21 years of age are also tax free.

    • Secure Future for Girl Child

      SSY investments can help secure the financial future of the girl child, since only parents of girl child can open this account and only 1 account per girl child is allowed.

    • Government-Backed Savings Scheme

      SSY is a government-backed savings scheme that offers assured returns and features low level of risk This provides SSY account with long-term growth potential that leverages the power of compounding.

    • Financial Support for Education and Marriage

      SSY account investments are designed to provide long-term savings to the girl child. The proceeds from the account can only be withdrawn to finance higher education and/or marriage of the girl child.

    • Affordable Investment Option

      The minimum investment required to keep the SSY account active is ₹250 annually and multiple investments in a tax year are allowed up to the limit of ₹1.5 lakh in a fiscal.

    13. Recurring Deposit (RD)

    [Recurring Deposit (RD)](/investment-plans/recurring-deposit) allow small investments to grow into a large corpus over time by allowing investors to benefit from the power of compounding. The interest rate offered by RDs is comparable to FDs of similar tenure. However, as per current tax rules there are no tax benefits of investing in recurring deposits.

    • Low-Risk Investment Option

      RD with banks in India are insured by the DICGC up to a limit of ₹5 lakh, while Post Office RD are backed by sovereign guarantee. This ensures that the principal invested is never at risk, making RD as one of the safest investment options in India.

    • Encourages Regular Savings

      RD investments are periodic in nature i.e. the same amount needs to be deposited on a monthly, quarterly or semi-annual basis. This can help inculcate a habit of saving and encourage financial discipline in the depositor.

    • Predictable Financial Growth

      Once fixed at the time of account opening, RD interest rates do not change over time. This means that the wealth creation of RDs remains predictable irrespective of how broader markets perform.

    14. Voluntary Provident Fund (VPF)

    Salaried individuals who have a EPF account can make additional contributions into the account via VPF. [Voluntary Provident Fund (VPF)](/blog/investments/voluntary-provident-fund) investments offer assured returns at the same rate as the underlying EPF account. VPF investments are eligible for tax benefits u/s 80C up to the cumulative limit of Rs. 1.5 lakh annually. The maturity of this investment is tied to the maturity of the underlying EPF account.

    • Higher Interest Earnings

      VPF deposits earn interest at the same rate as the EPF account. Currently, EPF interest rate is among the highest among government-approved fixed return schemes in India.

    • Long-Term Wealth Creation

      Individuals making VPF investments can stay invested till their retirement, typically at the age of 60 years. This means that the investments made will have significant time to grow by maximising the benefit from compounding.

    • Retirement Savings Growth

      Since VPF investments are linked to EPF, premature withdrawal options from the account are limited. This means that the compounding of this retirement-focused investment can continue without interruption.

    15. Kisan Vikas Patra

    This government-backed scheme doubles your investment after a pre-determined period based on the prevailing interest rate. The interest rate is subject to periodic revision by the Ministry of Finance. [Kisan Vikas Patra](/investment-plans/kvp-kisan-vikas-patra) currently does not offer any tax benefits, so the maturity amount and interest earned from this investment are subject to tax.

    • Capital Protection Benefits

      KVP investments in India are safe investments and the principal invested is never at any risk. This ensures that the investor’s capital is protected at all times.

    • Double Value at Maturity

      The KVP investment made grows to double the size of the original investment at maturity. When interest rates increase the time period to maturity of the certificate is shorter.

    • Trusted Small Savings Scheme

      KVP is a government-backed scheme that offers the safety of sovereign guarantee. This ensures that the investor can place their trust that the savings scheme will deliver the returns as expected.

    • Flexible Purchase Options

      Minimum KVP investment starts at ₹1000 and there is no maximum limit on the amount that can be invested in this scheme. This ensures flexibility for the investors and enables greater accessibility of the investment.

    16. Treasury Bills

    Treasury Bills, also known as T-Bills, are Government of India-backed debt instruments that are issued by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). T-bills typically mature within 1 year of issue, hence can be purchased by individual investors in India seeking short-term investments options. While the risk of these investments is considered a very low, as per current tax rules, these investments are not eligible for any tax deduction benefits. Additionally, the returns from T-Bills are also taxable as per applicable capital gains tax rules.

    • Government-Backed Security

      T-Bills are backed by the Government of India, so these offer the benefit of sovereign guarantee to investors. This makes T-Bills one of the lowest risk investment options currently available in India.

    • High Liquidity Benefits

      T-Bills can easily be traded on money markets. This ensures a high degree of liquidity to investors. What’s more as a result of their short maturity, even if held, the investor gets their investment back within a short time period.

    • No Market Volatility Impact

      T-Bills are issued for a period of 91 days, 182 days or 364 days. Due to their short duration, returns from this investment option is minimally impacted by changes in market interest rates or other types of market volatility.

    17. 5 Year Tax Saver FD

    [Tax Saving FDs](/investment-plans/fixed-deposit/tax-saving-fd) can be opened at various banks and in most aspects they are similar to other types of bank fixed deposits. Tax saving FDs mature in 5 years and the interest rate is locked in at the time of making the initial deposit. The investment qualifies for Section 80C tax benefits up to Rs. 1.5 lakh annually. The interest earned at maturity is taxable and subject to TDS as per applicable tax rules.

    • Market-Independent Returns

      Tax saver FD interest rates are fixed at inception and remain unchanged over the investment tenure. This means that the depositor earns returns from this investment option without any impact of changing market combination.

    • Wealth Preservation

      In most cases, returns offered by 5 year tax saver FD may be lower than market-linked investment made over a similar investment duration. However, the principal invested in tax saver FD are not impacted with changing market conditions.

    • Guaranteed Returns

      Tax saver FD returns are based on interest rates that are fixed at inception. This means that returns from the investment plan are guaranteed by the bank providing the deposit, irrespective of changes to the market interest rate.

    • Stable Interest Earnings

      5 year Tax Saver FD provides predictable growth of the investment made. This ensures stable interest earnings and predictable growth of investments till maturity of the FD.

    18. Mahila Samman Yojana

    A relatively recent short-term scheme backed by the government, this investment account can only be opened in the name of a woman or girl child. The maturity period is 2 years and there is no tax benefit of this investment plan.

    • Secure Savings for Women

      MSSC investments are only available to women investors and provides assured returns that are fixed at inception. This is designed to provide a secure savings route to women opting for this investment plan.

    • Attractive Interest Earnings

      MSSC features a high interest rate that is comparable to other popular small savings scheme in India. So, these attractive interest earnings can be availed with minimal risk to the capital invested.

    • Short-Term Investment Benefits

      MSSC investments mature in 3 years from the date of investment, after which the original investment along with interest can be withdrawn. Subsequently used for the intended purpose .

    • Stable and Guaranteed Returns

      MSSC offers returns at a fixed rate of interest, so the growth of investment is predictable and not impacted by changing market conditions.

    19. Money Market Funds

    Money Market Funds are a category of debt mutual funds that primarily invest in various money market instruments such as certificate of deposits, Treasury Bills, Repo agreements, call money, bills of exchange, etc. These fixed return instruments typically feature high liquidity and short maturity periods. So, money market funds are considered to have low interest rate risk and liquidity risk compared to many other types of mutual funds. While these funds are considered to be a relatively safe investment option compared to equities, returns from these funds are market-linked hence not guaranteed.

    • High Liquidity Benefits

      Money Market Funds do not feature a log-in period and primarily invest in securities that are traded on money markets. This ensures that investors can benefit from the high degree of liquidity offered by the investment plan.

    • Low-Risk Investment Option

      Money Market Funds invest in instruments that have relatively short maturities. This ensures that the investment plan is minimally impacted by interest rate changes, so the risk from this investment plan is minimised.

    • Low Volatility of Returns

      Money Market Funds are minimally impacted by changing market conditions such as interest rates. This ensures that the potential returns obtained from these funds are relatively less volatile as compared to many other types of mutual funds.

    20. Arbitrage Funds

    Arbitrage Funds are part of the broader equity mutual fund category. These funds exploit the price differences between the cash market and futures market of a stock to make profits. This funds tend to primarily invest in equity stocks as well as futures contract of corresponding equity stocks, hence are taxed as per equity mutual fund taxation rules. While execution of the arbitrage strategy may involve multiple simultaneous transactions in order to generate a profit, these are considered to be among the relatively low risk equity investments currently available in India.

    • Low-Risk Investment Strategy

      The arbitrage strategy utilised by these mutual funds is primarily designed to minimise investment risk in the face of changing market conditions. This ensures that Arbitrage Funds offer a low risk investment option to investors.

    • Stable Return Potential

      As a result of investing in both equity stock and matching equity derivatives, Arbitrage Funds can generate returns in case of both upward and downward movement of markets. This has the potential to delivery steady returns to investors irrespective of changing market conditions.

    • Tax-Efficient Investment Benefits

      Arbitrage Funds are categorised as equity investments from a taxation perspective. This ensures that returns from this investment plan features favorable tax treatment especially in the case of long term investment returns.

    21. Fixed Maturity Plan

    This is a type of closed-ended debt mutual fund scheme that primarily invests in fixed return instruments such as bonds, government securities, commercial papers, etc. However, unlike many other types of debt funds, FMPs typically do not trade securities on the bond or money markets. Instead all the debt securities included in the fund’s portfolio are held till maturity. This type of investment plan is considered suitable for investors seeking long-term investment in debt securities along with the benefit of professional fund management.

    • Better Return Visibility

      FMPs offer superior returns visibility as these invest in fixed-return instruments and these are held by the fund till maturity. So, even if returns are not guaranteed, investors have a clear idea about the potential returns they might receive from the FMP at maturity.

    • Suitable for Conservative Investors

      Since FMPs primarily invest in fixed return debt instruments, their returns tend to be less volatile than equities over various time periods. As a result, conservative investors tend to favour the predictability of these investments.

    • Reduced Interest Rate Risk

      FMPs usually hold their investments till maturity and do not engage in active trading of securities on the bond market or money market. This means that the performance of these mutual funds is minimally impacted by changes in market interest rates.

    22. Debt Funds

    [Debt Funds](/blog/investments/debt-funds) are a category of mutual funds that primarily invest in debt and money market instruments such as corporate bonds, Government Securities (G-Secs), Treasury Bills (T-Bills), Certificate of Deposits (CDs), etc. While these are considered less volatile as compared to equities, currently debt fund investments do not offer any tax saving benefits.

    • Lower Volatility

      Debt as an asset class tends to be less volatile as compared to equities. This benefit extends to debt funds which makes this investment plan and ideal instrument to diversify an equity-oriented investment portfolio.

    • Diversified Fixed-Income Exposure

      Typically debt funds invest in multiple fixed income instruments ranging from G-Secs and corporate bonds to various money market instruments and cash/cash equivalents. This ensures diversification of the debt fund portfolio.

    • Capital Preservation Potential

      During equity market corrections, debt markets typically witness a rise due to the benefit of negative cor-relation to equities. This ensures capital preservation in the investor’s overall portfolio in the case of a correction in equities markets.

    • Flexible Investment Options

      In the case of debt funds, there is often no minimum or maximum limits. This ensures that the investors can flexibly increase or decrease their debt fund investments according their financial capacity and goals.

Medium Risk Investment

23. Real Estate and Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs)

Residential or commercial property can be a smart investment choice for the long term. However, physical properties do come with significant initial costs and recurring costs related to maintenance. An alternative to purchasing physical properties is the purchase of [Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs)](/blog/investments/what-are-real-estate-investment-trusts-reits) units, which require relatively less initial investments but can generate passive income in the long term. As per existing rules, there are no tax benefits for making these investments.

  • Passive Income Generation

    Both commercial real estate and REITs investments can offer steady passive income through rental receipts or periodic interest and dividend payouts. This can provide alternative income to investors beyond their income from salary or business.

  • Real Estate Market Exposure

    These investment options offer the unique opportunity to gain exposure to the commercial real estate market. This can help diversify the current asset allocation in the investor’s portfolio.

  • Liquidity Benefits

    Unlike commercial real estate holdings, units of REITs are quite liquid and can easily be traded on stock markets where these units are listed. This provides much needed liquidity to investors that can come in handy in the case of a financial emergency.

  • Inflation Protection

    Historically real estate prices have shown a positive co-relation to inflation. So, as inflation rises with time, real estate prices tend to increase and hold their value against rising inflation.

24. Physical Gold (Bullion)

[Physical Gold (Bullion)](/blog/investments/how-to-invest-in-gold) in the form of jewellery, coins and bars, has been a popular investment choice since ancient times. While this investment can serve as a hedge during periods of uncertainty, there are no tax benefits of making these investments.

  • Hedge Against Inflation

    Gold has historically featured a positive co-relation with inflation. This has allowed gold investments to hold their value in the face of increasing inflation over long investment periods.

  • Tangible Asset Security

    Gold has intrinsic value unlike fiat currency such as printed bank notes. This is the key reason, physical gold is typically preferred by individuals seeking to protect themselves from potential economic turmoil.

  • Protection During Market Volatility

    Historically gold has been used to diversify investments especially in the case of equity-heavy portfolio. This is because gold tend to help manage overall portfolio volatility caused by equity investments over various time periods.

25. Corporate FDs

Corporate Fixed Deposits are a type of FD scheme that are offered and managed by various corporate entities. These are considered to be a more risky investment option compared to FDs offered by banks or the India Post Office. The risk profile of corporate FDs can be checked through risk ratings provided by various Credit Rating Agencies. Due to the perceived higher risk of corporate FDs, these tend to fetch a higher rate of return as compared to bank or post office FDs.

  • Higher Interest Rates

    Corporate FD do not feature insurance from DICGC, hence are considered to be riskier than bank deposits. As a result of this higher risk, corporate FDs typically offer higher interest rates than bank term deposits of comparable tenure.

  • Fixed and Predictable Returns

    Corporate FDs feature a fixed interest rate that remains unchanged over tenure of the deposit. This ensures fixed and predictable returns for investors who opt for these term deposits.

  • Suitable for Various Goals

    Corporate FDs are currently offered with various investment tenures. This means that these investment plans can be used to achieve a wide variety of short, medium and long term financial goals.

26. Unit Linked Insurance Plans (ULIPs)

Unit Linked Insurance Plans (ULIPs) are life insurance cum investment instruments that provides the dual benefit of life cover along with wealth creation. Suitable for investors with different risk appetites depending on the investment fund chosen, one gets Section 80C tax benefits up to Rs. 1.5 lakh annually on investment along with Section 10 (10d) benefits at maturity.

  • Market-Linked Wealth Growth

    ULIPs allow policyholders to invest in a wide variety of funds that invest in different market-linked or fixed-return instruments. So, ULIPs can help policyholders achieve a wide range of long-term goals depending on their risk appetite and choice of ULIP fund.

  • Long-Term Financial Planning

    ULIPs have a lock-in period of 5 years and typically do not allow withdrawals before completion of this time period. What’s more, ULIPs allow policyholders to make investments and stay invested for an extended period of time. This ensures suitability of ULIPs for long-term financial planning.

  • Option to Switch Between Funds

    ULIPs offers multiple fund options to choose from and policyholders can switch between funds as per terms and conditions specified by the fund. The option to switch between ULIP funds helps policyholders stay on course to reach their investment goals with relative ease.

27. Bonds

Bonds are fixed return instruments that are traded on bond markets. Bonds may be issued by various entities such as State Governments, Central Governments, financial institutions, private corporations, etc. As bonds are pure debt instruments they are typically impacted by changes in market interest rates. However bonds tend to be less volatile as compared to equities. Under current tax rules, bonds do not offer any tax deduction benefit on investments and returns from these investments are also taxable as per applicable Capital Gains Tax rules.

  • Stable Interest Income

    Bonds offer regular interest income based on a coupon rate that is specified at the time of bond issuance. These payouts provide regular income to the bond holder during the period of investment.

  • Lower Investment Risk

    Bond holders get preference to equity shareholders when the bond issuer makes payouts to investors. This helps reduce the potential risks associated with bond investments compared to equity investments in the same company.

  • Better Financial Stability

    Bonds tend to be less volatile than equities especially in the short-term. This can help improve the financial stability for bond holders especially during periods when equity markets are volatile.

28. National Pension System (NPS)

[National Pension System (NPS)](/blog/investments/nps-national-pension-scheme) allows subscribers to get market linked returns and create a corpus for retirement. Self-contributions into the NPS Tier 1 account allow tax deduction benefits of up to Rs. 2 lakh annually under Section 80C and Section 80CCD (1b)

Check Returns using [NPS Calculator](/calculators/nps-calculator) now!

  • Reduced Taxable Income

    NPS offers multiple tax deduction benefits both on self-contribution and employer contribution, These can help save tax primary for those opting for the old tax regime while also helping create a retirement corpus over time.

  • Diversified Portfolio

    NPS allows investments in 4 different asset classes – equity, G-Secs, corporate debt and alternative investment funds. This helps create a diversified portfolio that can potential reduce risk and maximise returns for the subscriber.

  • Age-Based Asset Allocation

    NPS allows subscribers to implement an age-based asset allocation strategy into their portfolio. This allows investors to maximise benefits of potentially high risk - high return investments such as equities at a young age. As the subscriber grows older, the allocation towards potentially less volatile instruments such as debt is increased to reduce overall portfolio risk.

29. Silver ETFs

Silver, though less popular as an investment compared to gold, is a precious metal that is traded on designated commodities exchanges. Relatively recently, exchange traded funds or ETFs that feature physical silver as the underlying asset have emerged as an investment choice for retail investors in India. This is a completely digital investment that can be bought and sold on the stock market, but its returns are primarily dependent on the price of silver. As per current tax laws, Silver ETFs do not offer any tax benefits to investors.

  • Ease of Investment

    Silver ETFs are offered through multiple routes such as RTAs, fund houses, stock markets, etc. This makes it easy to invest in and redeem silver ETF units with relative ease compared to physical silver.

  • No Physical Storage Hassles

    Silver ETFs can be stored safely in Demat accounts similar to equity stocks. This makes this investment option easy to store safely with significantly less hassle as compared to storing physical silver.

  • Inflation Hedge Potential

    Silver ETF returns are pegged to the changes in the price of physical silver. So as global and domestic silver prices move in tandem with inflation, the price of silver ETFs also increases. This ensures that your silver ETF investments hold their value over the long term.

30. Infrastructure Investment Trusts (InvITs)

Infrastructure Investment Trusts or InvITs are a type of alternative investment that feature the assets and revenues generated by infrastructure projects such as highways, bridges, etc. as the underlying asset. Through InvITs, investors can easily access the economic benefits of new and current infrastructure projects by potentially earning regular returns from the revenue generated by these projects. InvITs units are listed on stock exchanges and can be traded similar to equity shares, however, as per current tax rules, there are no tax deduction benefits of investing in InvITs.

  • Stable Passive Income

    InvITs are required to distribute at least 90% of their income to unitholders. This is usually paid out on a quarterly basis and can provide regular passive income to investors.

  • Diversified Infrastructure Exposure

    InvITs can invest in a variety of infrastructure projects such as roads, telecom, power generation/distribution, etc. So, this investment option can potentially provide access to a diverse range of infrastructure projects.

  • High Liquidity Advantages

    InvITs are listed on stock exchanges and can be traded similar to shares. This ensures ease of purchase and redemption of units which provides a high degree of liquidity to investor.

  • Inflation-Linked Growth Potential

    Infrastructure projects tend to increase in value as inflation drives up costs. This means that units of InvITs also have the potential to grow in value in line with rising inflation. This ensures that the value of investor’s savings grows with time.

31. Child Plans

[Child Plans](/child-insurance-plans) sold by life insurance companies provide Section 80C tax benefits and their maturity amount is also eligible for benefits under Section 10 (10d) of the Income Tax Act, 1961. Unlike Sukanya Samriddi, these plans are available to parents of both male and female children without restrictions.

  • Goal-Based Future Planning

    These savings plans are designed to achieve a specific goal such as ensuring sufficient funds to cover higher education or marriage expenses of a child irrespective of their gender.

  • Life Coverage with Savings Benefits

    Child plans allow market-linked growth of wealth to reach the goal of savings adequate funds for your child’s future. Beyond capital appreciation, this policy also provides financial security through life cover benefits.

  • Tax Free Returns at Maturity

    The returns from child plans are exempt from capital gains tax under Section 11 of Income Tax Act, 2025 (earlier Section 10-10d of Income Tax Act, 1961). This can help maximise returns from the plan.

32. Pension Plans

[Pension plans](/pension-plans) are long-term investment plans sold by life insurance companies are designed to provide regular income post retirement through monthly pension. Investments made into these schemes offer tax benefits u/s Section 80CCC up to the cumulative Section 80C limit of Rs. 1.5 lakh annually. The pension income is however taxable as per the [income tax slab](/blog/tax-savings/income-tax-slab-2026-27) rate of the individual.

  • Protection Against Inflation

    Pension Plans allow policyholders to invest in various market-linked funds. These funds are capable of generating inflation-beating long term returns that can help protect savings from the adverse impact of inflation.

  • Disciplined Retirement Savings

    Pension Plans require regular periodic investments on the part of the policyholder. This can help create financial discipline and inculcate a habit of saving in the policyholder.

  • Flexible Pension Payout Options

    Payouts from pension plans at maturity can be in the form of monthly income or a combination comprising an initial lump sum payout at maturity followed by monthly income. This flexibility helps provide additional financial security to the pension plan holder.

  • Stable and Secure Future Planning

    The primary goal of a pension plan is to act as a long-term financial planning tool and provide a financial safety net for the later years of life. This is usually achieved by ensuring predictable regular income post retirement.

33. Hybrid Funds

[Hybrid Funds](/blog/investments/what-are-hybrid-funds) are allowed to invest in both equity stocks and debt instruments. These schemes offer investors superior downside protection compared to pure equities and have the potential to give better long-term returns compared to debt schemes. But, as per current taxation rules, hybrid schemes do not offer any tax benefits on investments made and their returns are also subject to capital gains.

  • Equity and Debt Diversification

    Hybrid Funds invest in both equity and debt securities. So this investment plan helps investors diversify their investment portfolio without having to invest in multiple funds.

  • Balanced Risk and Return

    The diversified portfolio of hybrid plans provides a unique balance between the risk and return to the investor. This can help the investment plan outperform broader markets in a variety of market conditions.

  • Reduced Market Volatility Impact

    Equity markets tend to be volatile especially in the short-term. This is not the case with debt investments. So, the presence of debt instruments in the hybrid fund portfolio can help reduce the impact of equity market volatility on the investor’s overall portfolio.

34. Gold Funds, Gold ETFs and Digital Gold

These offer investors multiple ways to invest in and benefit from gold without having to worry about storing the physical gold. These also do not offer any tax deduction benefits to investors.

  • Higher Liquidity

    Gold Fund units can easily be redeemed with the issuing asset management company or on secondary markets such as the stock market. This gives gold fund investments higher liquidity as compared to physical gold.

  • Minimal Storage Costs

    Unlike physical gold, gold funds do not require lockers or secure storage. This makes gold fund units significantly cheaper to store safely. So the potential net returns from gold fund can be significantly higher.

  • Simplicity of Trading

    Gold Fund units do not have a mandatory lock-in period and can be freely traded on stock markets where they are listed. This ensures that gold funds can be traded easily as per the needs of the investor.

35. Fund of Funds

Funds of Funds are a type of mutual fund that primarily invests in units of an exchange traded fund (ETF) or a another mutual fund. These investment plans offer the same benefits as any other mutual fund such as diversification and professional fund management. While these funds tend to have a higher expense ratio that ETFs and other types of mutual funds, fund of funds often provide investors with access to diverse markets and industries in India and abroad that may not be available through other types of mutual funds.

  • Multi-Asset Diversification Benefits

    Fund of Funds have the option to invest in multiple mutual funds and ETFs simultaneously. This can help them create a diversified portfolio potentially featuring more than one asset class.

  • Professional Fund Management

    Fund of Funds offer investors access to professional fund management. This ensures than even novice investors can leverage the expertise of experienced professional fund managers to grow their wealth over time.

  • Access to Unique Investment Opportunities

    The fund-of-fund methodology Indian investors with access to a wide range of investment themes and opportunities. Many of these might not be available through the best investment plans currently available in India.

36. Residential Real Estate

Residential real estate can be a smart long-term investment considering the historical trend of rising property prices across India especially in the larger cities. Residential real estate in India can be in the form of residential land plot, standalone house or flats. While real estate investments often require significant initial costs and recurring maintenance costs, they can offer long-term passive income through rentals. One must keep in mind that residential real estate is typically only suitable as a long term investment and may offer limited liquidity if one attempts to sell residential property to raise funds in an emergency.

  • Long-Term Property Value Growth

    Historically residential property including residential plots, if held for the long term, have increased in value with time. This has resulted in higher demand for residential property which has resulted in further increase of property prices over time.

  • Stable Rental Income Opportunities

    If residential property is not self-occupied, it can be let out. This rental income can create a stream of passive income if a residential property is purchased specifically for the purpose if investment.

  • Tangible Asset Ownership

    Ownership of residential property acts as a tangible asset that can be used to passive income or used as collateral to obtain access to funds in an emergency. This is in addition to the potential growth of wealth that this investment option can offer in the long term.

  • Portfolio Diversification Benefits

    Adding real estate to an existing portfolio helps diversify investments by introducing a new asset class. This can help optimise potential returns and minimise the overall risk typically featured in the case of an investment portfolio.

37. Physical Silver

Silver can be used as an investment and historically its popular use has been limited to jewellery and coins. In recent years, with industrial applications driving silver demand higher, many more investors have started viewing silver bullion as a viable investment option in India. Physical silver can be bought and sold in the form of bars, coins as well as jewellery. A key benefit of investing in silver is the metal’s ability to provide a hedge against inflation. Moreover, the historical negative correlation of silver prices to equity prices also makes it a viable option to diversify equity-oriented investor portfolios.

  • Hedge Against Inflation

    Just like gold, silver also acts a potential hedge against inflationary pressure. This is because silver price in India and globally have managed to keep pace with inflation and managed to hold their value in line with rising inflation.

  • Portfolio Diversification Benefits

    Silver investments can act as an effective investment option to diversify an equity-oriented portfolio. This diversification can help optimise potential returns and reduce overall risk of the portfolio for investors.

  • Tangible Asset Security

    Silver has intrinsic value in its physical form and can be converted to cash in an emergency. What’s more as a precious metal, silver is also able to retains value and even grow in value during periods of economic turmoil.

High Risks Investments

38. Equity Mutual Funds

[Equity Mutual Funds ](/blog/investments/equity-mutual-funds)is a type of mutual fund that primarily invests in domestic equities and equity instruments. These can provide market-linked returns that have the potential to beat inflation in the long term, however only ELSS currently offers tax benefits to investors. So, in the case of other equity funds, returns are taxable as per applicable capital gains tax rules.

  • Higher Return Potential

    Equity mutual funds have historically demonstrated the ability to provide investors with inflation-beating long-term terms. This can help ensure that your savings are able to outpace with inflation over time.

  • Long-Term Capital Appreciation

    Equity mutual funds offer the benefit of compounding provided you stay invested in the long term. That’s why it is recommended that one stays invested in equity fund for periods of 5 years and greater to maximise potential benefits from the investment plan.

  • Professional Fund Management

    Equity mutual funds are managed by professional fund managers. This means that even novice investors have an opportunity to leverage the expertise of experienced fund managers by making these investments.

  • Goal-Oriented Wealth Building

    Long-term goals such as retirement, saving for children’s marriage, buying a residential house, etc. require one to stay invested for multiple years. Equity mutual funds are ideally suited for achieving such long-term goals.

39. Direct Equities

This allows investors to benefit from market-linked growth of shares that are traded on the stock market, however gains are subject to short term and long term capital gains tax rules.

  • Higher Return Potential

    Directly investing in multibagger stocks can give high returns, if you make the correct choice in stocks. However, to maximise the potential benefit you have to time your entry and exit perfectly.

  • Dividend Income Benefits

    Direct stock investments also gives you access to dividend payouts that might be made by the stock issuing company. This can provide you with interim income while you stay invested in the stock.

  • Market-Driven Growth Opportunities

    Performance of direct stocks depend on multiple factors ranging from market performance, industry outlook, growth potential of the company, etc. So, direct stock investments provide you with exposure to various market-linked growth opportunities.

40. Equity Linked Savings Schemes (ELSS)

[Equity Linked Savings Schemes (ELSS)](/blog/tax-savings/what-is-elss) is a type of equity-oriented mutual fund that features a 3-year lock-in period and provides market-linked returns to the investors. ELSS investments are eligible for Section 80C benefits up to the Rs. 1.5 lakh cumulative limit. However, redemption proceeds are taxable as per applicable mutual fund taxation rules.

  • Diversified Portfolio

    ELSS mutual funds feature a portfolio that is diversified across various market capitalisations and industries. This portfolio diversification ensures optimal returns and helps manage investment risk.

  • Tax Benefits

    ELSS is currently the only type of mutual fund in India that offers tax deduction benefit under Section 123 of Income Tax Act, 2025 (earlier Section 80C of Income Tax Act, 1961). This allows investors to claim tax deduction of up to ₹1.5 lakh in a tax year, if they have opted for the old tax regime.

  • Inflation-Beating Growth Potential

    Equity-oriented investments such as ELSS offer market linked returns that can potentially beat inflation over the long term. This can help investors use ELSS as a suitable option to achieve various long-term financial goals.

41. International Funds

This is a type of mutual fund that primarily invests in international equities. International Funds are considered by various investors as a simple and effective way to ensure geographic diversification of the investment portfolio. International Funds can focus on making investments either a single country or across multiple countries across a region to provide further diversification to investors. Since these funds do not typically hold domestic stocks in their portfolio, these funds are treated as debt funds from a taxation perspective.

  • Global Market Diversification

    By investing in International Mutual Funds, investors can diversify their portfolio and benefit from geographical diversification. This can potentially help the investor reduce investment risk and optimise overall returns from the investment.

  • Access to International Companies

    International Funds provide investors the opportunity to access unique themes and benefit from the potential growth of international companies. These benefits are typically not accessible via other investment options in India.

  • Reduced Currency Risk

    International funds provide Indian investors with access to growth in foreign currencies. This way, these investments can generate returns from the investor when the local currency weakens or the foreign currency becomes stronger.

42. Small Cap Stocks

In terms of market capitalization, listed stocks ranked 251 and lower are considered as small cap companies in India. These companies tend to be newer players who are in the process of growing their market share and often have relatively weak cash flows compared to larger companies. While their relatively small size tends to make them vulnerable to market downturns, small cap companies do offer higher long-term growth potential than midcap companies. In terms of potential risk, small cap companies are considered to be risker than midcap companies, so only limited portfolio allocation towards these stocks is recommended.

  • High Growth Potential

    Small cap companies have high growth potential especially in the long-term. This is because as they grow their business over time, they can grow to become mid cap and even large cap companies.

  • Long-Term Wealth Creation

    As small cap companies grow, they can increase the wealth of investors even if these are relatively risky due to their smaller size. This ensures that the investor’s wealth grows at a faster pace as compared to the growth in broader markets.

  • Access to Emerging Themes and Businesses

    Many Small cap companies are engaged in emerging themes and business models that have the potential to grow significantly large over time. These investments may not be accessible to investors via other investment options in India.

43. Midcap Stocks

In terms of market capitalization, midcap companies are smaller than their large cap counterparts. But, these stocks have the potential to emerge as future market leaders leading to a significant increase in future stock prices. While midcap stocks tend to be more volatile as compared to large cap stocks, they do have the potential to deliver superior long-term returns compared to large cap investment. This combination of high potential volatility and high returns makes midcap stocks a preferred investment option for investors with high risk appetite.

  • Long-Term Wealth Creation

    Midcap companies already have significant market share in their chosen field of operations and have the potential to emerge as market leaders over time. This means that investments in stocks of these companies have significant long-term growth potential for the investor.

  • Higher Growth Than Large Caps

    Large cap companies tend to be more stable and less volatile compared to midcap companies. However, the potential for further growth in case of midcap companies is typically much higher than large cap companies.

  • Portfolio Diversification Benefits

    Existing investors can incorporate midcap stock investments into their current portfolios to diversify their holding. This can help grow their investments faster as compared to broader markets over time.

44. Large Cap Stocks

Large cap stocks, also known to as blue chip stocks, refer to Indian companies that are ranked among the top 100 listed companies, on the basis of their market capitalization. These are well established companies and typically market leaders in their chosen sectors. Large cap stocks tend to have steady cash flows and are often well equipped to deal with changing market conditions. Due to their potential stability, these stocks are often preferred by investors who desire consistent long-term growth of their equity investments.

  • Consistent Long-Term Returns

    Large cap companies provide consistent long-term returns. This is because their leadership position in their chosen industry and strong cash flow provides significant protection against market corrections.

  • Lower Market Volatility

    Large cap stocks typically feature lower levels of volatility as compared to midcap or small cap stocks. This is because, changing market conditions tend to have only limited impact on the cash flow and profitability of these companies.

  • Superior Financial Stability

    Due to their leadership position in their chosen area of business, large cap companies tend to have strong cash flows, low levels of debt and a strong balance sheet. This provides superior financial stability to the business.

45. Index Funds

Index Funds are a category of passively managed mutual funds that attempt to the replicate the composition and performance of an underlying index within the margins of tracking error. Due to passive management, these funds provide unmatched transparency with respect to stock selection and portfolio construction that eliminates the potential risk of fund manager bias. Additionally, while index funds might not be able to outperform their underlying index, their low fund management charge, versus actively managed funds, can result in high net returns for investors.

  • Broad Market Diversification

    Since index fund replicate the composition of a specific index, this investment plan can feature a portfolio that is well diversified across various market capitalisations and industries/sectors.

  • Passive Investment Strategy

    Index funds follow a rules-based and transparent passive investment strategy. This ensures that the choice of investments is not impacted by potential fund manager bias.

  • Minimal Fund Management Costs

    Index Funds, being passively managed, feature low portfolio churn and fewer purchase/sale transactions. This leads to a lower expense ratio and result in potentially higher net returns for the investor.

  • Transparent Investment Structure

    By following a rules-based investment strategy, this investment plan ensures that stock picks are made in a transparent manner. This feature makes Index Funds one of the most transparent investment options in India.

46. Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs)

[Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs)](/blog/investments/what-are-exchange-traded-funds-etfs) are a unique type of mutual fund that can be traded on stock exchanges similar to equity stocks. ETF can provide market-linked returns to investors however, currently they do not offer any tax saving benefits.

  • Low-Cost Investment Benefits

    ETFs are passively managed financial instruments, so these tend to have relatively low fund management charges. This means that net returns from ETFs can be significantly higher that many other professionally managed top investment plans.

  • Easy Market Diversification

    ETFs tend to have well-diversified portfolio of underlying investments. So, ETFs can help you diversify your current investment portfolio with minimal effort.

  • Passive Wealth Building Strategy

    ETFs are passively managed investment plans that follow a transparent, rules-based investment process. This ensures that ETF investments are free from fund manager bias.

  • Tax-Efficient Investment Option

    ETFs primarily invest in equities so their returns are treated similar to returns from other equity investments. This makes ETFs a tax efficient investment options especially if one stays invested for the long term.

View Plans

How Do Investment Plans Work?

To make an informed decision regarding which investment plan would suit your unique need, you need to have a clear understanding of how investment plans work. Below are some key steps in the working of a best investment plan:

1

Set Your Investment Goals

Typically, investment plans are ideally suited to achieving specific goals that have to be achieved within a specific time period. For instant, NPS investments are designed to be suitable for the long-term goal such as saving for retirement, while a high yield savings account is considered ideal for maintaining an emergency fund. So, before choosing any specific investment plan, pay close attention which goal you want to achieve and whether the unique features of the investment plan align with your goal.

2

Pick a Suitable Investment Plan

Once you have a clear goal in mind, you need to consider the potential risk associated with the investment before making your choice. Every investment option features some degree of risk. So, a specific plan can be considered suitable only if the inherent risks associated with the investment align with the level of risk you are comfortable with. For instance, stocks may be ideal for a long-term investor with a high risk appetite, but this will not be suitable for conservative long-term investor seeking guaranteed returns.

3

Start Investing

No matter how solid your investment plan is, you will only be achieve your financial goal if you follow the plan. So, actually starting your investment journey is crucial and an early start is desirable to maximise potential compounding benefits from your investments. So, instead of delaying your investments seeking cheaper valuations or waiting till you earn more, start your investments as early as possible.

4

Grow Your Money Through Compounding

The importance and impact of compounding on your long-term investments cannot be overstated. The key to maximising this benefit is time. The more time your investments have to grow, the greater the benefit of compounding that you can receive over time. So, start early and stay disciplined with your investments so that the benefit of compounding can help you create a substantial investment corpus over time.

5

Track and Optimize Your Portfolio

In order to stay on track to reach your goal, periodic assessment of the performance of your overall portfolio as well as individual investment plans is essential. This can help you identify potential underperformers and rebalance your portfolio periodically to align with your financial needs as well as changing market conditions. This way you are in a position to better manage the overall risk in your portfolio and potentially optimise returns in order to stay on track to reach your investment goals.

6

Withdraw

Once you reach your planned goal, it is time to start planning withdrawal so that the funds can be used for their original purpose. You can choose to withdraw the entire corpus as a lumpsum, generate monthly income or opt for a mix between the two depending upon the need.

For example, a corpus created for the purpose of purchasing a house might be withdrawn as a lump sum in its entirety, if you are purchasing a ready to move in flat. On the other hand, a systematic monthly payout may be preferred if you are seeking to replace your regular income after retirement.

How to Choose the Best Investment Plan?

Follow these steps to build a strategy aligned with your goals, risk profile, and timeline.

1

Assess Your Financial Needs & Goals

Before starting your investment, understand why you want to make it. You may have goals like retirement planning, your child’s education, or buying a home. Having clear goals helps you select the best investment plans that truly support your priorities.
2

Calculate Your Investment Timeline

Every financial goal has a different time horizon. Short-term goals need safer options, and long-term goals need growth-focused plans. Thus, you must choose a timeline that matches with your goals. It helps you balance risk and returns effectively.
3

Build a Strategy Using the Right Insurance + Investment Mix

A strong financial plan combines protection with wealth creation. For instance, insurance plans help secure your family, while the best investment plans in India build long-term assets. With this diversified approach, you can keep your finances prepared for uncertainties like inflation.
4

Diversify Your Portfolio

Relying on a single investment increases financial risk. So, spreading funds across different investment and insurance plans reduces dependency. If one plan fails to payout due to any discrepancy, other plans will be helpful.
5

Understand All Charges

Every investment scheme comes with certain charges. These may include fund management fees or administrative costs. The best investment plans always provide a transparent fee structure. Knowing these helps you estimate actual returns more accurately.
6

Review Your Investment Plans Regularly

As your income, goals, and responsibilities change over time, periodic reviews of your portfolio become helpful. It ensures your investments stay aligned with these changes. Thus, you should adjust your investment plans promptly.
7

Choose by Your Risk Comfort

Not everyone is comfortable with market ups and downs. Some prefer stability, while others are open to taking calculated risks. Thus, choose a plan that lets you stay invested without constant worry.
8

Consider Liquidity Needs

Life can bring unexpected expenses at any time. It can be any emergency medical expenses or other liabilities. Thus, it’s vital to ensure some investments that allow for easily accessible funds. Also, avoid locking all your savings into long-term instruments.
9

Balance Returns with Stability

High returns often come with higher uncertainty. Though there are uncertainties, the best investment plans support growth with stability. Thus, you should choose the plans that follow this approach. It will help you stay consistent during market changes.

      Why Choose Axis Max Life Investment Plans?

      Here are the reasons why you should choose investment plans from Axis Max Life Insurance.

      1. Solutions for Different Life Stages

      Whether you are starting your career or planning retirement, Axis Max Life offers investment plans for every phase of life. It helps you invest with clarity and confidence.

      2. Protection Along with Wealth Growth

      The best investment plans from Axis Max Life offer both investment benefits and life cover. This ensures your family stays financially supported. Moreover, your money grows consistently.

      3. Flexible Payment and Benefit Choices

      Axis Max Life allows you to choose premium payment terms as per your convenience. Moreover, you can decide whenever and how benefits will be received. This flexibility helps manage cash flow better.

      4. Clear and Transparent Pricing

      We offer a 100% transparent pricing structure. So, we disclose all charges upfront. We don't have any hidden costs that affect long-term returns.

      Smart Investment Ideas to Maximise Wealth

      Maximising wealth creation is often considered as one of the key goals of investing. But not every investment strategy or investment option might be suited to your unique financial needs. This is in part because, as every top investment plan carries some degree of risk, you need to strike a balance between risk and potential returns to stay on course to achieve various financial goals. Below mentioned are some key ways that you can stay on track to maximise wealth creation from your investments:

      • Balance Risk with Equity and Debt Instruments

        Equities are a popular investment option among those seeking to maximise wealth creation over the long term. However, equities are considered as risky investments since their performance is prone to volatility especially in the short term. Debt instruments, on the other hand, might offer limited capital growth, but they tend to be less volatility than equities. So, a portfolio that is diversified across these two asset classes is better equipped to handle market volatility to provide an optimal balance between risk and returns.

      • Focus on Long-Term Wealth Creation

        When making investments, it is always advisable to take the long-term view instead of trying to maximise short-term returns. By staying invested over the long term, you can maximise the benefit of compounding and reach your wealth creation goals with greater ease. On the other hand, if you try to chase short-term returns, you may end up trying to change your investments too frequently. This can adversely impact long-term wealth creation.

      • Explore Market-Linked Investment Opportunities

        If you are investing for the long-term, you have to factor in the impact of inflation. Since rise in inflation leads to a decrease in net returns from your investment, you have to consider options that can providing inflation beating long-term returns. One way to achieve this is by opting for market linked investment plans such as equity-oriented investment options. These investments can help your savings keep pace with inflation so that you can stay on track to reach your long-term investment goals.

      • Manage Risk Through Strategic Asset Allocation

        Asset allocation typically involves making investments in different asset classes, so that your portfolio outperforms the broader markets irrespective of changing market conditions. In strategic asset allocation, the investor changes portfolio exposure to different asset classes in order to benefit from changes in the broader market. For instance, when equity markets undergo a correction, you may decide to increase your equity exposure and decrease your debt allocation. This can help your portfolio post higher returns when equity markets recover at a later date.

      1Year
